title: Manauli Fort
tagline: Manauli Fort — heritage city of Punjab

overview.short: Manauli Fort, also known as Quila Manauli, is a fortress (qila) located in the middle of the Manauli hamlet in Mohali district, Punjab, India.
overview.description: Manauli Fort, also known as Quila Manauli, is a fortress (qila) located in the middle of the Manauli hamlet in Mohali district, Punjab, India. It is 11 kilometres (7 mi) from Mohali city and Chandigarh and nearly 4 km away from International Chandigarh Airport Road Mohali. Manauli is a small hamlet with a population of 3,919 and 693 households (2011 Census). As per land record area of the village is 738 hectares (1,820 acres) (s.no.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Daun kalan
topPlaces.description: Daun kalan is a village in Patiala district, India. The Daun Kalan village has population of 3482 of which 1868 are males while 1614 are females as per Population Census 2011.

topPlaces.name: Bahadurgarh Fort
topPlaces.description: Bahadurgarh Fort is a historical Sikh-fort near Patiala in Punjab, India. Constructed on the site of the old Saifabad Fort, which was built as the residence of Nawab Saif ud-Din Mahmud or Saif Khan, the fort was renovated by Maharaja Karam Singh of the princely state of Patiala in 1837.

topPlaces.name: Rurki Kasba
topPlaces.description: Rurki Kasba is a census town in Patiala district in the Indian state of Punjab.

topPlaces.name: Gurdwara Dukh Nivaran Sahib
topPlaces.description: Gurdwara Dukh Nivaran Sahib is situated in what used to be the village of Lehal, now part of Patiala city.

topPlaces.name: Patiala
topPlaces.description: Patiala (Punjabi pronunciation: [pʌʈeɑ̈ːɭɑ]) is a city in southeastern Punjab, northwestern India. It is the fourth largest city in the state and is the administrative capital of Patiala district.

topPlaces.name: Shri Kali Devi Temple, Patiala
topPlaces.description: Shri Kali Devi Temple is a Hindu temple in Patiala, Punjab, India. It was built by the Maharaja of Patiala in 1936.

topPlaces.name: North Zone Cultural Centre
topPlaces.description: North Zone Cultural Centre or NZCC (ISO: Uttara Kṣētra Sāṁskr̥tika Kēṁdra) in Patiala in Punjab state was first among several regional cultural centres established by Government of India to preserve and promote arts, crafts, traditions and cultural heritage of the Indian states of Punjab, Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Haryana, Rajasthan, as well as the union territories of Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh and Chandigarh. Establishment of North Zone Culture Centre was announced by then Prime Minister of India, Rajiv Gandhi during his visit to Hussainiwala, Punjab on 23 March 1985.

topPlaces.name: Qila Mubarak, Patiala
topPlaces.description: Qila Mubarak, is a fortress of Sikh architecture in Patiala, Punjab, India. It was the residential palace of the Maharaja of Patiala.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Manauli Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Manauli Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Manauli Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chandigarh International Airport (47 km) and the nearest railway station is Ambala Cantt Junction (39 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Manauli Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Manauli Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Manauli Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Manauli Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Manauli Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Daun kalan, Bahadurgarh Fort, Rurki Kasba, Gurdwara Dukh Nivaran Sahib, Patiala.
